data.title: Weyerhaeuser declares $0.21 dividend
data.description:
• Weyerhaeuser (WY) declares $0.21/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.11%
  • Payable March 20; for shareholders of record March 10; ex-div March 10.
  • See WY Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Medical Properties Trust declares $0.09 dividend

• Medical Properties Trust (MPT) declares $0.09/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 6.47%

data.title: Cementos Pacasmayo S.A.A. reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Cementos Pacasmayo S.A.A. press release (CPAC): Q4 Revenues increased by 6.2%, in line with the increased sales volume mentioned above.
  • Consolidated EBITDA, without the transaction-related expenses, was S/ 158.7 million, an 11.4% increase. Including these expenses, consolidated EBITDA decreased to S/81.1 million.
  • Net income, excluding the transaction-related expenses, was S/ 59.8 million, a 19.6% increase. Including these expenses, net income turned to a net loss of S/ 17.8 million.

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM CPI 

The CPI is a measure of the change in the average price level of a fixed basket of goods and services purchased by consumers.

The consensus looks for stable inflation readings with CPI up 0.3 percent for total and core on the month and up 2.5 percent from a year ago for both total and core.

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs. Used for drilling wellbores for wells that may eventually produce oil or gas, active rigs are essential for the exploration and development of oil and gas fields.

data.title: Agnico Eagle beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Agnico Eagle press release (AEM): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.69 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $3.56B (+59.6% Y/Y) beats by $110M.
  • Payable gold production in the fourth quarter of 2025 was 840,608 ounces at production costs per ounce of $1,113, total cash costs per ounce of $1,089 and AISC per ounce of $1,517. The higher realized gold price of $4,163 per ounce in the fourth quarter resulted in strong margins and cash flows, while increasing royalty costs. 
  • The Company generated cash provided by operating activities of $2,112 million or $4.22 per share and record free cash flow of $1,310 million or $2.62 per share.
  • The Company increased its net cash3 position to $2,670 million as at December 31, 2025 as a result of the increase in its cash balance by $511 million to $2,866 million during the quarter and total debt outstanding as at December 31, 2025 of $196 million.

  • The 2026 production and cost guidance summary is set out below.

data.title: Galiano Gold beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Galiano Gold press release (GAU): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.15 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $156.68M (+142.7% Y/Y) beats by $33.68M.
  • The Company sold 38,276 ounces of gold in Q4 2025 at a quarterly record average gold price (before the effect of realized hedging losses) of $4,164/oz for gross revenue of $159.7 million.
  • Reported Adjusted EBITDA1 of $85.5 million in Q4 2025, compared to $21.2 million in Q4 2024.
  • The Company generated $55.8 million of cash flow from operating activities in Q4 2025, compared to $13.8 million in Q4 2024.

  • FY 2026 Guidance

    • For FY 2026, gold production guidance at the AGM is forecast between 140,000 ounces and 160,000 ounces. Higher mined grades are expected to be progressively mined from Abore over the course of the year, therefore gold production is forecast to be weighted to the back half of FY 2026. FY 2026 gold production is forecast to increase approximately 25% compared to FY 2025.

    • AISC1 guidance for FY 2026 is forecast between $2,000/oz and $2,300/oz at a gold price assumption of $4,500/oz and under the current royalty framework in Ghana.

data.title: Air Canada Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.65, revenue of C$5.77B; initiates Q1 and FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Air Canada press release (ACDVF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.65.
  • Revenue of C$5.77B (+6.9% Y/Y).
  • Operating income of C$324 million with an operating margin of 5.6% and adjusted EBITDA of C$867 million with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 15.0%.
  • Adjusted CASM* of 15.34 cents
  • Net cash flows from operating activities of C$423 million and free cash flow of negative C$478 million.

  • Outlook

    For the first quarter of 2026, Air Canada plans to increase its operated capacity by about 2.5 per cent from the same quarter in 2025.

    Air Canada is providing the following guidance for the full year 2026.

    Metric 2026 Guidance
    Adjusted EBITDA C$3.35 billion to C$3.75 billion
    ASM capacity 3.5% to 5.5% increase versus 2025
    Adjusted CASM 15.05 ¢ to 15.35 ¢
    Free cash flow C$400 million to C$800 million

data.title: Mattel CEO Ynon Kreiz buys $1.01M in shares at $15.53 apiece
data.description:
• Mattel (MAT) CEO Ynon Kreiz acquired 65,000 shares of company stock at an average price of $15.5277 per share, boosting his total holdings to 1,794,217 shares, according to a recent regulatory filing.
  • The company's shares fell earlier this week after missing estimates for the crucial holiday quarter. Net sales were up 7% year-over-year to $1.77B in Q4, or 5% on a constant currency basis for the quarter that ended on December 31. The increase in sales as reported was driven by a 5% increase in North America and an 11% increase internationally.

data.title: Instacart signals 11%-13% GTV growth guidance for Q1 2026 amid expanding enterprise and AI momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Maplebear Inc. (CART) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Chris Rogers, Chairman, CEO & President, highlighted that "we closed out the year with our strongest GTV growth in 3 years, ads and other revenue grew 10% year-over-year. And based on our strong conviction in how the business is performing, we repurchased $1.1 billion worth of shares in Q4 alone." He detailed the company's strategic focus on being "the platform consumers trust for all of their grocery needs, provide the technology grocers rely on to power their omnichannel business and be the advertising ecosystem brands prefer on Instacart and across many other services."
  • Rogers underscored the company's advancements in generative AI, stating, "AI is no longer about just about making teams faster or more productive. We're seeing fundamental shifts in how work gets done and how platforms create advantage."
  • Marketplace expansion was also emphasized with "more than 2,200 retail banners spanning nearly 100,000 locations are accessible on the Instacart app or instacart.com."
  • On enterprise, Rogers explained, "we now power more than 380 grocery e-commerce sites, and we see a lot of runway ahead, both to launch with new partners and to expand with existing partners as they adopt more of our solutions."
  • CFO Emily Maher stated, "GTV was $9.85 billion, up 14% year-over-year, marking our strongest growth in 3 years. This performance was driven by orders reaching $89.5 million, up 16% year-over-year."
  • Maher also addressed financial discipline: "GAAP net income was $81 million, down 46% year-over-year. This decline was primarily due to higher G&A expenses related to nonrecurring legal and regulatory matters, including a $60 million settlement with the FTC. Absent these nonrecurring expenses, Q4 GAAP net income would have increased year-over-year."

Outlook

  • Maher guided for Q1 GTV to range between $10.25 billion to $10.275 billion, representing "year-over-year growth between 11% to 13%, with GTV growth expected to outpace orders growth as we lap the full launch of our $10 minimum basket feature for Instacart Plus members in the prior year quarter."
  • She projected Q1 adjusted EBITDA of $280 million to $290 million, with advertising and other revenue expected to grow 11% to 14% year-over-year.
  • For fiscal 2026, Maher stated, "we remain committed to steady annual adjusted EBITDA year-over-year growth at a rate that outpaces GTV growth. Similar to prior years, we expect this rate of expansion to moderate year-over-year as we reinvest to accelerate across our multiple growth engines."

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EBITDA grew 20% year-over-year to $303 million, and operating cash flow was $184 million, a 20% increase.
  • The company noted share repurchases totaling $1.4 billion in 2025, with $1.1 billion in Q4 alone.
  • Transaction revenue represented 7.1% of GTV, and advertising and other revenue outperformed expectations despite macro uncertainty among large brand partners.
  • Payments to publishers contributed to higher cost of revenue, with Maher noting, "while payments to publishers scale throughout 2025, we expect its year-over-year growth to moderate in 2026."

Q&A

  • Douglas Anmuth, JPMorgan: Asked about marketplace vs. enterprise opportunity and the balance between new retailer onboarding and expansion with existing partners. Rogers responded that "both of those have been growing and both are healthy," emphasizing significant room for expansion, especially internationally.
  • Nikhil Devnani, Bernstein: Questioned drivers behind strong GTV and user growth, and competition. Rogers cited strong user growth, engagement, and retention, adding "none of the competition activity that we're seeing surprises us at all given the size of the market opportunity for online grocery."
  • Charles Larkin, Oppenheimer: Inquired about advertising performance. Rogers attributed strength to GTV growth, diversification, and scaling Carrot Ads, noting "Carrot Ads expanded to over 310 partners... over 9,000 brands advertising on Instacart."
  • Shweta Khajuria, Wolfe Research: Asked about international expansion and local commerce. Rogers explained their disciplined approach, focusing on leveraging current enterprise technology in Europe and Australia, and reaffirmed primary focus on grocery.
  • Josh Beck, Raymond James: Sought insight on price parity and AI-driven experiences. Rogers said, "We consistently see price parity retailers outperform marked up retailers on Instacart. We see better retention price parity retailers."
  • Multiple analysts probed on advertising pipeline, AI partnerships, and cost structure. Maher clarified that off-platform ads are a smaller but growing segment and that publisher payments' impact on margins is expected to moderate.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a probing yet constructive tone, frequently focusing on competitive threats, advertising trends, and international expansion. Questions pressed management on sustainability of growth, margin trajectory, and strategic investments, with some skepticism toward competitive dynamics and the impact of large brand ad spend volatility.
  • Management's sentiment was confident and optimistic, especially in prepared remarks. Rogers used phrases such as "we are confident," "we feel good about our points of differentiation," and "I'm confident in our ability to compete in this market in the long run." During Q&A, management remained assertive, emphasizing resilience and growth levers, while occasionally repeating earlier points for emphasis.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was more assertive about AI and international expansion, while analysts maintained a similar focus on competition and growth levers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 guidance for GTV growth (11%-13%) showed an acceleration compared to the 9%-11% in Q3.
  • Management increased emphasis on AI implementation, reporting engineering output up nearly 40% and production-grade software build times more than four times faster.
  • Share repurchase activity intensified in Q4 compared to Q3, with $1.1 billion of shares repurchased.
  • Advertising and other revenue growth guidance for Q1 2026 (11%-14%) is higher than the prior quarter’s 6%-9% range.
  • The competitive landscape and price parity initiatives continued as central themes, but management signaled more confidence in their differentiation and international expansion prospects.
  • Analyst questions remained consistent, probing competition, margin sustainability, and the effects of macroeconomic trends.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified macro uncertainty among large brand partners as a constraint on advertising growth.
  • Nonrecurring legal and regulatory expenses, including a $60 million FTC settlement, impacted GAAP net income.
  • Payments to publishers have increased cost of revenue, but Maher expects the growth in these payments to moderate in 2026.
  • Analysts raised concerns over competition from Amazon and DoorDash, international expansion risks, and balancing growth with profitability.
  • Management reiterated commitment to disciplined investment and expanding share in underpenetrated international markets while maintaining profitability.

Final Takeaway

Maplebear Inc. concluded 2025 with record GTV growth and order momentum, driven by expanding marketplace scale, robust enterprise partnerships, and accelerated AI adoption. The company projects 11%-13% GTV growth for Q1 2026, reinforced by double-digit advertising and other revenue growth guidance. Management remains confident in its multi-engine growth strategy, disciplined financial execution, and ability to extend category leadership while navigating competitive and macroeconomic pressures.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Sensus Healthcare targets profitability in 2026 with expanded reimbursement and diversified customer base
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sensus Healthcare (SRTS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Joseph Sardano, Co-Founder, Chairman & CEO, opened the call highlighting the company's "wonderful news received from CMS"—exclusive and dedicated CPT codes for SRT and IG-SRT technology, ensuring clear reimbursement for non-melanoma skin cancer treatments. Sardano emphasized, "After 16 years of relentless pursuit, these codes provide Sensus with a fresh start, a clear path forward for physicians and patients who continuously seek a noninvasive alternative to scarring and the lengthy healing times caused by surgery."
  • Sardano reported, "We begin in 2026 with new codes, $22 million in cash on hand, 0 debt and a motivated sales force that we intend to expand during the course of Q1." He noted that the 14 units shipped in the quarter did not include any sales to their largest customer and that future growth is expected from direct sales and shared services, reducing the risk of customer concentration.
  • The Fair Deal Agreement program ended the year with 18 active sites and 10 pending activation, with treatments up more than eightfold versus 2024 and patient numbers increasing by more than 250% during 2025.
  • International business showed momentum with 6 systems shipped in Q4, including to China, and international sales contributing positively to margins.
  • Michael Sardano, President and Chief Commercial Officer, explained, "Reimbursement certainty and highly attractive economics have expanded adoption pathways for SRT... Customers now have multiple ways to adopt our technology, and we are able to support Fair Deal Agreements, ownership, renting or leasing depending on practice needs."
  • Javier Rampolla, Chief Financial Officer, stated, "Revenues for the fourth quarter of 2025 were $4.9 million compared with $3.1 million in the fourth quarter of 2024."

Outlook

  • The company expects Q1 2026 system shipments to exceed Q4 2025 levels, even without contributions from its historically largest customer. Joseph Sardano stated, "Based on our current pipeline and customer engagement, we expect first quarter system shipments to exceed fourth quarter levels even without any contribution from our historically largest customer."
  • The objective for 2026 is to achieve full year profitability, with management emphasizing, "our objective is to achieve full year profitability in 2026."
  • Management plans to expand the sales force by hiring 3 to 5 new representatives as soon as possible to boost market education and lead conversions.

Financial Results

  • Revenues for Q4 2025 were $4.9 million. Cost of sales was $3 million. Gross profit was $1.9 million or 38.8% of revenues. General and administrative expenses were $1.8 million, and selling and marketing expenses were $1.4 million. Research and development expenses were $1.9 million.
  • Net loss for Q4 2025 was ($3.2 million) or a loss of ($0.19) per share. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was negative $3 million.
  • Full year 2025 revenues were $27.5 million, with cost of sales at $15.6 million and gross profit at $11.9 million or 43.3% of revenues. Net loss for the year was ($7.7 million) or a loss of ($0.47) per share. Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 was negative $9.6 million.
  • The company ended the year with $22.1 million in cash and no outstanding borrowings. Inventories totaled $14.6 million, and prepaid inventory was $1.5 million.

Q&A

  • Anthony Vendetti, Maxim Group: Asked if guidance for Q1 revenue growth assumes no contribution from the largest customer and about orders from them in 2026. Joseph Sardano responded, "we made sure that we didn't include any expectations from our biggest customer... everything that we're going to be projecting for 2026 excludes them for the moment."
  • Vendetti inquired about TDI product approval. Joseph Sardano answered, "we're working closely with the FDA and continuing to work through the situation that they seem to lack an understanding. And so I don't know when that's going to happen, but we're going to continue to pursue it wherever we possibly can."
  • Vendetti asked about international demand. Michael Sardano replied, "China has been our bread and butter internationally for years... Taiwan and how that's been growing... Asia, in general, continues to adopt SRT technology... we're holding out hopes for the Middle East as well... we expect to get Brazil clearance this year."
  • Benjamin Haynor, Lake Street Capital: Asked about the impact of reimbursement changes on system mix. Joseph Sardano explained, "the FDA still remains a priority for all of the private equity-backed roll-up groups... single customers... are seeing a much stronger influence regarding the actual purchase of the equipment or actually going to a lease."
  • Haynor also asked about interest from private equity-backed groups. Sardano indicated, "they're very seriously reevaluating how they want to acquire it."
  • Haynor inquired about Sentinel 2.0. Sardano said, "we're looking at a more broad rollout for it. And I think that we'll see these things rolling out in the near future."
  • On service revenue, Javier Rampolla stated, "service revenue still is like 10% of the total revenue for the company."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts appeared optimistic about the impact of new reimbursement codes, international expansion, and product pipeline, but pressed for details on major customer contributions and regulatory progress. Questions about guidance, international momentum, and product approval reflected a slightly positive but probing tone.
  • Management maintained a confident, upbeat tone throughout the call, using phrases like "we are encouraged by early activity" and "we are able to support Fair Deal Agreements, ownership, renting or leasing depending on practice needs." In Q&A, management was transparent about uncertainty with the largest customer and regulatory timelines, but reiterated confidence in core business growth.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone was slightly more positive, with greater focus on forward growth prospects. Management’s tone shifted from cautious optimism to greater confidence due to the successful acquisition of dedicated CPT codes and a more diversified customer base.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from cautious optimism about new CPT codes and market demand in Q3 to firm expectations of profitability and growth in 2026, excluding the largest customer’s contributions.
  • Strategic focus has moved from awaiting reimbursement clarity to scaling sales, expanding internationally, and diversifying the customer base.
  • Key metrics such as unit shipments decreased sequentially (14 in Q4 vs. 16 in Q3), but revenue increased due to higher international sales and Fair Deal Agreement placements.
  • Management’s confidence increased in Q4, citing "a fundamentally different operating environment" and a clearer path to profitability, compared to a more tentative outlook in Q3.
  • Analysts in Q4 focused on sustainability of growth, product pipeline, and international ramp, whereas in Q3, questions centered more on reimbursement risk and utilization trends.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified ongoing uncertainty with the largest customer, stating projections for 2026 exclude their contribution.
  • Regulatory uncertainty persists regarding TDI product approval, as "the FDA... seem to lack an understanding."
  • International expansion remains dependent on obtaining regulatory clearances in key markets such as Brazil.
  • Higher servicing and placement costs associated with the Fair Deal Agreement program contributed to lower gross margins and negative EBITDA.

Final Takeaway

Sensus Healthcare enters 2026 with dedicated CPT codes for SRT and IG-SRT, a strong cash position, and a diversified customer base. Management aims for full year profitability, driven by expanded reimbursement, increased direct and international sales, and a disciplined approach to scaling sales. While challenges remain with regulatory approvals and customer concentration, the company projects improved visibility, operational leverage, and growth potential in the year ahead.
